question: How many artists are in the database?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Artist;

question: How many tracks are in the database?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Track;

question: What is the title of the album with AlbumId 1?
gold_sql: SELECT Title FROM Album WHERE AlbumId = 1;
gold_answer: For Those About To Rock We Salute You

question: How many customers are from Brazil?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Customer WHERE Country = 'Brazil';

question: How many employees does the company have?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Employee;

question: List all media type names in alphabetical order.
gold_sql: SELECT Name FROM MediaType ORDER BY Name;
gold_answer:
Name
AAC audio file
MPEG audio file
Protected AAC audio file
Protected MPEG-4 video file
Purchased AAC audio file

question: What is the duration in milliseconds of the longest track?
gold_sql: SELECT MAX(Milliseconds) FROM Track;

question: How many invoices were billed to Germany?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Invoice WHERE BillingCountry = 'Germany';

question: What is the total revenue across all invoices?
gold_sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(Total), 2) FROM Invoice;

question: How many distinct billing countries appear on invoices?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT BillingCountry) FROM Invoice;

question: What is the average track length in milliseconds, rounded to the nearest whole number?
gold_sql: SELECT CAST(ROUND(AVG(Milliseconds)) AS INTEGER) FROM Track;

question: How many albums does the artist Iron Maiden have?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Album a JOIN Artist ar ON a.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId WHERE ar.Name = 'Iron Maiden';

question: Which 5 countries have the most customers? Return country and customer count, highest first.
gold_sql: SELECT Country, COUNT(*) AS Customers FROM Customer GROUP BY Country ORDER BY Customers DESC, Country LIMIT 5;
gold_answer:
Country | Customers
USA | 13
Canada | 8
Brazil | 5
France | 5
Germany | 4

question: Which genre has the most tracks? Return the genre name and its track count.
gold_sql: SELECT g.Name, COUNT(*) AS Tracks FROM Track t JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId GROUP BY g.GenreId ORDER BY Tracks DESC, g.Name LIMIT 1;

question: Which 3 artists have the most tracks? Return artist name and track count, highest first.
gold_sql: SELECT ar.Name, COUNT(*) AS Tracks FROM Track t JOIN Album al ON t.AlbumId = al.AlbumId JOIN Artist ar ON al.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId GROUP BY ar.ArtistId ORDER BY Tracks DESC, ar.Name LIMIT 3;
gold_answer:
Name | Tracks
Iron Maiden | 213
U2 | 135
Led Zeppelin | 114

question: How many customers does each support rep look after? Return the rep's full name and customer count, alphabetical by name.
gold_sql: SELECT e.FirstName || ' ' || e.LastName AS Rep, COUNT(*) AS Customers FROM Customer c JOIN Employee e ON c.SupportRepId = e.EmployeeId GROUP BY e.EmployeeId ORDER BY Rep;
gold_answer:
Rep | Customers
Jane Peacock | 21
Margaret Park | 20
Steve Johnson | 18

question: What are the 4 highest invoice totals? Return invoice id and total, highest first.
gold_sql: SELECT InvoiceId, Total FROM Invoice ORDER BY Total DESC, InvoiceId LIMIT 4;

question: How many tracks exist per media type? Return media type name and track count, most tracks first.
gold_sql: SELECT m.Name, COUNT(*) AS Tracks FROM Track t JOIN MediaType m ON t.MediaTypeId = m.MediaTypeId GROUP BY m.MediaTypeId ORDER BY Tracks DESC, m.Name;
gold_answer:
Name | Tracks
MPEG audio file | 3034
Protected AAC audio file | 237
Protected MPEG-4 video file | 214
AAC audio file | 11
Purchased AAC audio file | 7

question: Who are the top 5 customers by total spend? Return the customer's full name and their total spend rounded to 2 decimals, highest first.
gold_sql: SELECT c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName AS Customer, ROUND(SUM(i.Total), 2) AS TotalSpent FROM Invoice i JOIN Customer c ON i.CustomerId = c.CustomerId GROUP BY c.CustomerId ORDER BY TotalSpent DESC, Customer LIMIT 5;
gold_answer:
Customer | TotalSpent
Helena Holý | 49.62
Richard Cunningham | 47.62
Luis Rojas | 46.62
Hugh O'Reilly | 45.62
Ladislav Kovács | 45.62

question: What are the top 5 genres by total sales revenue? Return genre name and revenue rounded to 2 decimals, highest first.
gold_sql: SELECT g.Name, ROUND(SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity), 2) AS Revenue FROM InvoiceLine il JOIN Track t ON il.TrackId = t.TrackId JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId GROUP BY g.GenreId ORDER BY Revenue DESC, g.Name LIMIT 5;
gold_answer:
Name | Revenue
Rock | 826.65
Latin | 382.14
Metal | 261.36
Alternative & Punk | 241.56
TV Shows | 93.53

question: Which artists have more than 10 albums? Return artist name and album count, most albums first.
gold_sql: SELECT ar.Name, COUNT(*) AS Albums FROM Album al JOIN Artist ar ON al.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId GROUP BY ar.ArtistId HAVING COUNT(*) > 10 ORDER BY Albums DESC, ar.Name;
gold_answer:
Name | Albums
Iron Maiden | 21
Led Zeppelin | 14
Deep Purple | 11

question: Which billing countries have total invoice revenue above the average per-country revenue? Return country and revenue rounded to 2 decimals, highest first.
gold_sql: SELECT BillingCountry, ROUND(SUM(Total), 2) AS Revenue FROM Invoice GROUP BY BillingCountry HAVING SUM(Total) > (SELECT AVG(country_total) FROM (SELECT SUM(Total) AS country_total FROM Invoice GROUP BY BillingCountry)) ORDER BY Revenue DESC, BillingCountry;
gold_answer:
BillingCountry | Revenue
USA | 523.06
Canada | 303.96
France | 195.1
Brazil | 190.1
Germany | 156.48
United Kingdom | 112.86

question: Using a ranking over track counts, which album(s) hold rank 1 by number of tracks, and how many tracks do they have?
gold_sql: SELECT Title, Tracks FROM (SELECT al.Title AS Title, COUNT(*) AS Tracks, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS rnk FROM Track t JOIN Album al ON t.AlbumId = al.AlbumId GROUP BY al.AlbumId) WHERE rnk = 1 ORDER BY Title;

question: How many customers spent more than the average customer's lifetime spend?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT CustomerId, SUM(Total) AS spent FROM Invoice GROUP BY CustomerId) WHERE spent > (SELECT AVG(per_customer) FROM (SELECT SUM(Total) AS per_customer FROM Invoice GROUP BY CustomerId));
